title: 9 Troubleshooting Tracing Requests
body_text:
9 	Troubleshooting Tracing Requests
9.1	Why Customers CanГўВҖВҷt Send or Receive
Interac
ГӮВ®
e-Transfer Transactions
Disabled Account
Is the customerГўВҖВҷs profile status ГўВҖВңDisabledГўВҖВқ? Customers canГўВҖВҷt send or receive transfers while their profile is disabled. Check their Customer Details page in OAS and look for the Disabled indicator. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: The Disabled Account Indicator
Scrubbed Email Address
Is their email address ГўВҖВңscrubbedГўВҖВқ? Customers canГўВҖВҷt send or receive transfers through an email address that is currently on the
Interac
ГӮВ®
Scrubbed Email list. Check their Customer Details pageГўВҖВҷs Profile Summary tab of the Customer Details page in OAS. Look for a Scrubbed Email field displayed under their email address, accompanied by a Revoke option. See Figure 2.
Figure 2: Scrubbed Email Indicator
Note: 	Pending transfers that were sent before an email address was scrubbed are blocked when that email address has been placed on the scrubbed list.
Exceeding a Transfer Limit
Is the customer at or near their limits for transfers? Customers canГўВҖВҷt send or receive transfers that are over limit. Check the Profile Summary tab of their Customer Details page in OAS to determine what limits are currently in place for the customer for sending transfers. See Figure 3.
Figure 3: Customer Interac e-Transfer Limits
A customerГўВҖВҷs transfers are subject to a maximum amount per individual incoming or outgoing transfers, or to a limit on the total amount of outgoing transfers that can occur over a defined time period, known as a rolling limit. Rolling limits are calculated by adding up all the transfers occurring between the current date/time and the date/time that is exactly (calculated to the minute) 1 day, 7 days, or 30 days ago (depending on the specific rolling time period). Under a rolling limit, room for sending additional transfers becomes available when older transfers drop out of the calculations.
Note: 	There are no rolling limits on incoming transfers.
Interac
has set a maximum per limit of $25,000 on any incoming transfer. This limit isnГўВҖВҷt shown on the customerГўВҖВҷs Profile Summary as it is a global limit set by
Interac
and applicable to all customers of all institutions.
Interrupted
Interac
e-Transfer
ГӮВ®
Service
Is the
Interac
e-Transfer service running and available?
Customers canГўВҖВҷt send or receive transfers while the e-Transfer service is down
.
Check the
Interac
Services tab on the Transfer Parameters tab of the OAS Home page. Look for a current maintenance window advisory. Check if the system Heartbeat is active, indicating that
Interac
services are up and running. See Figure 4.
Figure 4: The Transfer Parameters Page
Blocked Email Address / Blocked Mobile Phone
Is the customerГўВҖВҷs notification channel on a Blocked Email Address list or a Blocked Mobile Phone list? Customers canГўВҖВҷt send or receive transfers while their notification channel is on
Interac
ГўВҖВҷs Blocked Email Address list or Blocked Mobile Phone Number list. Check with a Fraud Analyst to see if the customerГўВҖВҷs notification channel is on a blocked list, as there is no indication in OAS that indicates if it is.
InteracГўВҖВҷs
blocked lists are only visible to Fraud Analysts.
Open Transfers
Does an open transfer already exist between the sender and the recipient? Customers canГўВҖВҷt send transfers to a recipient while another transfer to them remains open
.
Perform a customer search in OAS and look for an open transfer between the customer and the recipient in the customerГўВҖВҷs transaction history. See Section 4.3, Tracing
Interac
e-Transfer Transactions in OAS.
Note:	An open transfer might include an interrupted transfer. See Interrupted Transfers below.
Blocked Transfers
Was the transfer blocked? A transfer is blocked by
Interac
or by a Fraud Analyst if it is suspected to be fraudulent. Check the blocked status of the transfer on the Transfer Details page in OAS. Check the Transfer History section of the Transfer Details to confirm who blocked the transfer. Look for a transfer memo under the Details column that might describe why the transaction was blocked, if blocked by a Fraud Analyst. See Figure 5.
Figure 5: The Transfer Details Page Blocked Transfer Indicator
Cancelled Transfers
Was the transfer cancelled? A transfer is cancelled by a Fraud Analyst if it is determined to be fraudulent. Check the status of the transfer on the Transfer Details page in OAS. Check the Transfer History section of the Transfer Details to confirm who cancelled the transfer. Look for a transfer memo under the Details column that might describe why a Fraud Analyst performed a cancellation. See Figure 6.
Figure 6: The Transfer Details Status Indicator
Interrupted Transfers
Was the transfer interrupted? An interrupted transfer is a transfer that has been left in an inconsistent state due to a breakdown or timeout during communications with your banking host or
Interac,
or due to the transfer being abandoned (i.e., left unconfirmed) by the customer. The transfer may or may not appear in OAS, depending on what stage the transfer was in when it was interrupted.
Interrupted transfers are displayed on the Interrupted Transfers tab on the OAS Home screen. Interrupted transfers also appear on the Customer Details page of the sending customer, as shown in Figure 7.
Figure 7: Customer Details ГўВҖВ“ Interrupted Transfers
Corrections of interrupted transfers are managed by CentralВ 1. Where applicable, Central 1 has automated processes that clean up certain interrupted transfers. When automated processes do not apply, Central 1 either rolls back transfers or moves them forward to completion, depending on the outcome of an investigation into whether a transfer has gone through the customerГўВҖВҷs bank account.
Failed Transfers
Did the transfer fail to complete? Check the ECCX Failed Transactions reports the day after the transfers occur. Use the
Interac Ref No
or the
C1 Retrieval Reference No
as listed on the report as search values to search OAS for the transfers.
To search by
Interac Ref No
, use the search category
Interac Payment Reference
. The value of the reference number must be entered exactly as it appears on the ECCX report, as searches by this category are case-sensitive and do
not
accept trailing wildcards.
To search by
C1 Retrieval Reference No
, use the search category
FI Reference Number
. Searches by this category are
not
case-sensitive and do accept trailing wildcards.
If the transactions can be found in OAS and are at status ГўВҖВң
Completed
ГўВҖВқ, make any manual adjustments to your customerГўВҖВҷs banking account necessary to complete the transfer on the banking side. If the transactions are
not
found in OAS, reverse any amounts out of your customerГўВҖВҷs bank account that were posted in settlement of the transaction.
Misdirected Funds
Under Account Number Routing (ANR), a new feature being introduced under
Interac
e-Transfer for Business, senders of transfers can send a transfer by providing the bank account information of the recipient (Institution #, Branch #, and Account #) without necessarily providing the recipientГўВҖВҷs email address or mobile phone number. Funds sent under ANR can theoretically be misdirected to the wrong institution, branch, or account. Transfers can also fail because of bad account details.
If your customer is the sender and the transfer failed, a failed transaction will show on your ECCX Failed Transactions report (see Failed Transactions above). If the transfer was misdirected to the wrong financial institution, branch, or account, have CentralВ 1 initiate a request for the return of the misdirected funds. If you have received misdirected funds, CentralВ 1 can respond to requests for the recovery of funds under the new Misdirected Funds Recovery tab on the OAS Home page. At the time of the preparation of this document, no ANR transactions are being sent through the
Interac
e-Transfer service; but they will soon begin appearing as the phasing in of
Interac
e-Transfer for Business enhanced send capabilities is completed.
END
